channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Welcomes Supreme Court Ruling on OBC Creamy Layer and Calls for Fair Implementation
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin has welcomed the Supreme Court’s recent verdict on the 'creamy layer' for the Other Backward Classes (OBC) reservation. The apex court clarified that parental inc...

item.title: Nelson Mandela Institute joins hands with Global Education Link to boost research and innovation
item.description: ARUSHA: The Nelson Mandela African Institute of Science and Technology don seal Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Global Education Link Ltd. The main goal of dis partnership be to strengthen rese...

item.title: Trump Criticizes Obama and Biden on Stair Climbing During Rally
item.description: During a rally in Hebron, Kentucky, President Donald Trump drew attention to what many considered a trivial complaint about former President Barack Obama’s way of descending stairs. Trump remarked to ...

item.title: Microsoft and Meta commit billions to expand data centre infrastructure amid AI growth
item.description: Microsoft and Meta Platforms have each committed nearly US$50 billion (around A$70 billion) in new data centre leases in their latest financial quarters, signalling a significant escalation in investm...

item.title: High Court Temporarily Halts NTSA Instant Fines System Pending Review
item.description: The High Court in Nairobi has issued a temporary suspension of the National Transport and Safety Authority's (NTSA) newly introduced Instant Fines Traffic Management System. This came after Sheria Mta...

item.title: Full Closure of 102 Avenue Planned for Edmonton’s Valley Line West LRT Construction
item.description: The City of Edmonton has announced that 102 Avenue will be completely closed to vehicle traffic from 107 Street to 102 Street starting March 16, 2026, to accommodate ongoing construction on the Valley...

item.title: Government investigates elevated hydrogen sulphide levels causing odour in Gauteng
item.description: The Department of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment is actively monitoring reports of a strong sulphur-like odour detected across parts of Gauteng, particularly in Johannesburg and Ekurhuleni. T...

item.title: President Marcos Emphasizes Peace and Unity During Grand Iftar 2026
item.description: President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. joined Muslim leaders and community members at the Grand Iftar 2026 held at Malacañang Palace, highlighting the importance of peace both locally and globally. In his ...

item.title: Security shoots attacker dead at Michigan synagogue amid US-Israel-Iran tensions
item.description: On Thursday, an unidentified gunman was killed after he opened fire at Temple Israel synagogue located on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan. Law enforcement officials responded swiftly, and after exc...

item.title: Vivian Reddy commits R100 million to grow KZN’s care economy and create jobs
item.description: Businessman and philanthropist Vivian Reddy has pledged R100 million over four years to develop KwaZulu-Natal’s care economy, highlighting the potential for social impact and job creation. Speaking at...

item.title: 17-year-old girl shot in legs in targeted incident in Liverpool's Toxteth area
item.description: A 17-year-old girl was shot in the legs during an incident that Merseyside Police have described as a targeted attack in Toxteth, Liverpool. The shooting occurred at an address on Lime Grove, off Lodg...

item.title: Nigeria Police Redeploy Two DIGs: Achinyan to Force Intelligence Department, Onwuemelie to Logistics and Supply
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force don confirm say dem don redeploy two big oga Deputy Inspectors-General (DIGs) to new positions inside the force. According to internal wireless message wey dem mark as 'Most I...

item.title: Zanzibar uses visitor insurance fees to support health services and visitor care
item.description: Zanzibar government, through the Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar (RGZ), collects around 1 million US dollars monthly from mandatory travel insurance for all visitors. Introduced on October 1, 202...

item.title: Iranian Attacks on Middle East Energy Infrastructure Push Oil Prices Back Above $100
item.description: Oil prices have surged past the $100 per barrel mark due to attacks on Middle East energy infrastructure, mainly attributed to Iran. Brent crude briefly reached $101 after Iranian strikes targeted two...

item.title: Mayon Volcano Records Ash Emission and Lava Fountaining Events, Remains at Alert Level 3
item.description: Mayon Volcano in Albay province exhibited renewed activity yesterday, as reported by the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs). At 4:20 a.m., a brief lava fountaining episode o...

item.title: Dennis Richardson resigns from Bondi massacre inquiry, increasing pressure on royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson, a highly experienced former public servant and former head of ASIO, has resigned from his role investigating security matters linked to the Bondi massacre within the antisemitism ro...

item.title: India Examines Energy Supply Requests from Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Maldives Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: On March 12, 2026, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) of India confirmed that it has received formal requests from Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Maldives for energy supplies, specifically diesel. MEA...

item.title: Suspect killed in vehicle ramming and shooting at Michigan synagogue; no civilian casualties reported
item.description: On 12 March 2026, an armed suspect deliberately drove a truck into Temple Israel, a large Reform Jewish synagogue in West Bloomfield Township, Michigan, which includes an attached preschool and early ...

item.title: PM Modi Discusses West Asia Tensions and Regional Peace with Iranian President
item.description: On 12th March 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a conversation with Iranian President Dr. Masoud Pezeshkian to discuss the growing tensions in West Asia, particularly the ongoing conflict involv...

item.title: Public and private schools in Islamabad observe holiday on March 13
item.description: All public and private educational institutions in Islamabad, including schools, colleges, and universities, will remain closed on March 13, 2026, following a notification issued by the district magis...
